---
title: "geseca_analysis_atac"
---

Date: Dec 8 2025 


Based on 5_Xenium_giotto_analysis, do GESECA analysis 
Load in the raw gene score matrix (and maybe imputed matrix) and do geseca analysis and compare to the xenium results and see

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({ 
library(fgsea)
library(Seurat)
packageVersion('Seurat')
library(arrow, lib.loc = '/projectnb/paxlab/presh/giotto_libs')
library(SummarizedExperiment)
})

path <- "/projectnb/paxlab/presh"
```


```{r}
genescore <- readRDS(file.path(path, 'gene_score_deepseq.rds'))
# read in interested cells 
int.cells <- read.csv('/projectnb/paxlab/presh/Data/int_cells_trim_atac.csv')
int.cells.new = paste0('Deepseq#',int.cells$x,'-1')

cells_indices_from_names_sparse <- match(int.cells.new, colnames(genescore))
subset_sparse_genescore <- genescore[, cells_indices_from_names_sparse, drop = FALSE]

gene_score_matrix <- assay(subset_sparse_genescore)

rownames(gene_score_matrix) = genescore@elementMetadata@listData[["name"]]
# Extract cell metadata (colData) and convert to a standard data.frame
cell_metadata <- as.data.frame(colData(genescore))

# --- Step 2: Create a new Seurat object ---
# When using CreateSeuratObject, the 'counts' argument expects the primary expression matrix.
# Since gene_score_matrix holds scores (already processed/normalized),
# we'll put it here, but then explicitly move it to the 'data' slot in the next step.
# It's good practice to provide dimnames.
seurat_obj_genescore <- CreateSeuratObject(
  counts = gene_score_matrix, # Temporarily place scores here
  meta.data = cell_metadata,
  assay = "GeneScores", # Name your new assay (e.g., "ATAC", "GeneActivity", "GeneScores")
  project = "ATAC_GeneScore_Project" # Optional: give your project a name
)

```

Processing the spatial data 
```{r}
coords <- spatial_locs <- read.csv(file.path('/projectnb/paxlab/yeting/SpatialATACseq/data',
                                   "/tissue_positions_list.csv"),
                         header = FALSE)
colnames(spatial_locs) <- c("barcode", "in_tissue", "array_row", "array_col", "x_spatial", "y_spatial")

xy <- spatial_locs[, c("barcode", "x_spatial", "y_spatial")]
xy2 = spatial_locs[spatial_locs$in_tissue == 1, ]

# if anything is above y spatial = 4000 it is tissue 488 , else 487 
xy2$tissue = ifelse(xy2$y_spatial > 4000, 'Tiss_488B', 'Tiss_487')

# subset to int cells 
rownames(xy2) <- xy2$barcode
xy3 <- xy2[int.cells$x,]
xy3$barcode2 = paste0('Deepseq#',xy3$barcode,'-1')

coords = xy3[, c("x_spatial", "y_spatial", "barcode2")]
colnames(coords) = c('x', 'y', 'cell')

dummy_image_data <- matrix(0, nrow = 1, ncol = 1)
fov_object <- CreateFOV(
  coords = coords,
  type = "centroids", # Explicitly state the type of coordinates
  key = 'fov1',
  image = dummy_image_data
)


# Add spatial coordinates to metadata 
# check if order is the same 
colnames(seurat_obj_genescore) == xy3$barcode2 
rownames(seurat_obj_genescore@meta.data) == xy3$barcode2 

```

Keeping only protein coding genes
```{r}
library(AnnotationDbi)
library(EnsDb.Hsapiens.v79)

edb <- EnsDb.Hsapiens.v79 # Use your specific EnsDb version

current_seurat_genes <- rownames(seurat_obj_genescore)

    # Query the EnsDb database for gene biotypes
    gene_info <- AnnotationDbi::select(
     edb,
     keys = current_seurat_genes, # Use the cleaned gene IDs
     keytype = "SYMBOL",
     columns = c("GENEID", "GENEBIOTYPE", "SYMBOL") # Also retrieve SYMBOL for verification
    )

    # Filter for protein-coding genes
    protein_coding_genes_edb <- gene_info %>%
      filter(GENEBIOTYPE == 'protein_coding') %>%
      pull(SYMBOL) %>%
      unique() # Ensure unique gene IDs

    # Get the intersection with genes actually in the Seurat object
    protein_coding_genes_to_keep <- intersect(current_seurat_genes, protein_coding_genes_edb)
    
```



```{r}

seurat_obj_genescore@images[["fov1"]] <- fov_object

seurat_obj_protein_coding <- subset(seurat_obj_genescore, features = protein_coding_genes_to_keep)


# xobj.seu <- subset(xobj.seu, subset = nCount_Xenium > 50)
# xobj.seu.crop <- xobj.seu[, xobj.seu$x > 5000 & xobj.seu$x < 10600]

# Normalize and scale data
seurat_obj_protein_coding <- NormalizeData(seurat_obj_protein_coding, normalization.method = "LogNormalize", scale.factor = 1000, verbose = FALSE)
seurat_obj_protein_coding <- FindVariableFeatures(seurat_obj_protein_coding, nfeatures = 2000, verbose = FALSE)
seurat_obj_protein_coding <- ScaleData(seurat_obj_protein_coding, verbose = FALSE)

# # Perform PCA and UMAP dimensionality reduction
# seurat_obj_genescore <- RunPCA(seurat_obj_genescore, verbose = FALSE)
# seurat_obj_genescore <- RunUMAP(seurat_obj_genescore, dims = 1:20)
# seurat_obj_genescore <- FindNeighbors(seurat_obj_genescore, reduction = "pca", dims = 1:30)
# seurat_obj_genescore <- FindClusters(seurat_obj_genescore, resolution = 0.2)


ImageDimPlot(seurat_obj_protein_coding, 
                   dark.background = T, size = 0.8, 
                   flip_xy = F) 


# ImageDimPlot(
#   seurat_obj_genescore,
#   fov = "fov1", # Specify the FOV you just added
#   dark.background = TRUE,
#   size = 0.5,
#   flip_xy = FALSE
# ) +
#   ggplot2::coord_flip() +
#   ggplot2::scale_x_reverse()


# DimPlot(seurat_obj_genescore,  raster = FALSE, pt.size = 0.5, stroke.size = 0.0) + 
#      ggplot2::coord_fixed()
```


```{r}
library(fgsea)
library(msigdbr)
set.seed(1)


seurat_obj_protein_coding <- RunPCA(seurat_obj_protein_coding, verbose = F, rev.pca = TRUE, reduction.name = "pca.rev", 
               reduction.key="PCR_", npcs=30)

E <- seurat_obj_protein_coding@reductions$pca.rev@feature.loadings

gsets <- msigdbr(species="human", category="H")
gsets <- split(gsets$gene_symbol, gsets$gs_name)


gesecaRes <- geseca(gsets, E, minSize = 10, maxSize = 500, center = FALSE, eps = 1e-100)

head(gesecaRes, 10)

topPathways <- gesecaRes[, pathway] |> head(4)
titles <- sub("HALLMARK_", "", topPathways)
```


```{r}
topPathways <- gesecaRes[, pathway][1:4]
titles <- sub("HALLMARK_", "", topPathways)

imagePlots <- plotCoregulationProfileImage(
    gsets[topPathways], object = seurat_obj_protein_coding, 
    dark.background = FALSE, 
    title = titles, 
    size=0.9, minLimit = -2, maxLimit = 2
)

imagePlots <- lapply(imagePlots, function(p){
    suppressMessages(
        p +  ggplot2::coord_flip() 
    )
})

cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = imagePlots, ncol = 2)

```
